reasoning: The document shows significant structural failure with the reported omission of at least nine specific slides and critical illustrative images. While programmatic signals show high text recall, the LLM fidelity score of 0.50 and specific evidence of content linearization and missing sections indicate a failure to capture the document's informative visual nature. Given the high cost of false negatives for government accessibility standards, this requires manual intervention.

concerns.analysis: The HTML output fails to represent the visual organization of the source PDF slide deck. The presentation relies on side-by-side comparisons (e.g., pages 12, 18, 23, 26) and visual examples to communicate its message. The HTML conversion linearizes everything into a single column of text and, critically, completely skips entire sections/slides that were purely visual (e.g., slides 12, 13, 15, 18, 20, 21, 23, 25, 26 are missing from the content flow). concerns.source_pdf_quote: The source PDF contains complex comparative layouts on pages 12, 18, 23, and 26, and informative visual-only slides on pages 13, 15, 20, 21, and 25.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML output completely omits these comparison slides and sections, reducing the 29-page presentation to a truncated list of text headings and bullet points. Side-by-side comparative layouts are lost.

concerns.analysis: Almost all meaningful illustrative images and screenshots are missing from the HTML output. These are not decorative background images; they are the primary content of the presentation, providing visual proof of the website improvements discussed in the text. action_items: Verify if the content from slides 12, 13, 15, 18, 20, 21, 23, 25, and 26 exists in the HTML or was entirely dropped.
Determine if missing images (like the WAVE tool results and phone evolution) can be recovered with proper alt-text or if they are permanently lost.
Evaluate if the linearization of side-by-side comparisons on pages 12, 18, 23, and 26 maintains logical reading order.
